summary: Official current-user runtime consent-store surface for denying location access. Microsoft documents both HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CapabilityAccessManager\ConsentStore\location\Value and the NonPackaged companion path as REG_SZ values that can be set per user. The app now writes Deny to those same two paths only.

setting.name: Disable Location Consent (Current User)
setting.casual_explanation: This changes the current user's location permission state to deny access for packaged and desktop apps.
setting.why_people_change_it: People use this when they want a strict per-user location denial without relying only on broader device policies.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This can effectively deny location access for the current user, but location-aware apps and services may stop working correctly until the consent state is restored.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Azure Virtual Desktop location-redirection guidance documents the exact current-user CapabilityAccessManager consent-store paths for packaged and NonPackaged location access and shows Value as the runtime consent field. The source publishes Allow as the enable example. The app now writes Deny to those same paths and no longer performs the extra ShowGlobalPrompts companion write that previously kept this record in review.

allowed_values.label: Normal user consent flow
allowed_values.meaning: The current user follows the normal Windows consent behavior for location.

allowed_values.label: Allow location access
allowed_values.meaning: The current user's location consent store explicitly allows access for packaged apps.

allowed_values.label: Deny location access
allowed_values.meaning: The current user's location consent store explicitly denies access for packaged apps.

allowed_values.label: Normal desktop-app consent flow
allowed_values.meaning: Desktop apps follow the normal Windows consent behavior for location.

allowed_values.label: Allow desktop-app location access
allowed_values.meaning: The current user's location consent store explicitly allows access for desktop apps.

allowed_values.label: Deny desktop-app location access
allowed_values.meaning: The current user's location consent store explicitly denies access for desktop apps.

app_current_implementation.notes: The provider writes Deny to the two documented current-user consent-store paths only: the packaged location path and the NonPackaged companion path.

states.rationale: Leave the packaged-app runtime consent value absent and let Windows manage the normal current-user consent behavior.
Leave the desktop-app runtime consent value absent and let Windows manage the normal current-user consent behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: People who want an explicit per-user location deny state

states.rationale: Leave the packaged-app consent-store value absent.
Leave the desktop-app consent-store value absent.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the normal Windows consent flow.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a hard current-user deny state for location.
tradeoffs: Does not explicitly deny location access.

recommended_profiles.label: Deny location access
intended_for: Users who want a strict per-user location deny state
avoid_for: Users who rely on location-aware apps and services

states.rationale: Use the documented packaged-app consent-store path to deny location access.
Use the documented NonPackaged consent-store path to deny desktop-app location access.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want an explicit current-user deny state for location access.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want apps and services to keep using location normally.
tradeoffs: Packaged and desktop apps lose location access for the current user.
Location-aware features may stop working until consent is restored.
Reversible by deleting the values or setting them back to Allow.

evidence.title: Microsoft location consent runtime guidance for current-user access
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the exact current-user CapabilityAccessManager location consent-store paths and their Value field for packaged and NonPackaged location access.

evidence.title: Current privacy provider location-consent batch
evidence.summary: The provider writes Deny into the current-user location consent store and the NonPackaged location consent subkey.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the exact current-user CapabilityAccessManager consent-store paths and Value field for location access, including the NonPackaged user path. The app now writes only those documented surfaces, so the mismatch is resolved.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Enable Let apps access your location (this value needs to be set per user): Key: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CapabilityAccessManager\ConsentStore\location; Type: REG_SZ; Value name: Value; Value data: Allow. Enable Let desktop apps access your location: Key: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CapabilityAccessManager\ConsentStore\location\NonPackaged; Type: REG_SZ; Value name: Value; Value data: Allow.
validation_proof.notes: This is an official current-user runtime consent surface for location access, not a Group Policy-backed LocationAndSensors setting.
